content.text:
You are implementing a full-stack engineering deliverable and must build it so it can pass a strict static-only reviewer audit.

Project package root must be created exactly as:

TASK-w2t76/
├─ docs/
│  ├─ design.md
│  ├─ api-spec.md
│  ├─ questions.md
│  ├─ test-coverage.md
│  └─ reviewer-notes.md
├─ repo/
│  ├─ frontend/
│  ├─ backend/
│  ├─ docker-compose.yml
│  ├─ run_tests.sh
│  ├─ README.md
│  ├─ unit_tests/
│  └─ API_tests/
├─ sessions/
│  ├─ develop-1.json
│  ├─ develop-2.json
│  └─ bugfix-1.json
└─ metadata.json

All packaged content must be in English only.

The business prompt is:

"Construct a FleetReserve Operations Suite supporting offline vehicle fleet scheduling, e-ticket admissions, and auditable multi-role access for a local rental yard or dealership service center. The Leptos web UI runs in a browser on the facility network and presents a unified availability calendar that operators can switch between day/week views, filter by location and asset status, and create reservations for vehicles and related service bays in 15-minute increments within configurable business hours (default 7:00 AM–7:00 PM). When a user proposes a booking, the interface immediately explains conflicts in plain language (overlapping reservation, vehicle in-repair hold, expired insurance, or capacity exceeded) and suggests the nearest two alternative time slots and, when applicable, an alternate eligible vehicle or bay. Roles are explicit and task-oriented: Customers can sign in and view their upcoming reservations and tickets, Photographers can access only assignments tied to their jobs, Merchants/Store Staff can manage local fleet assets and reservations for their store, Platform Operations can manage cross-store calendars and exports, and Administrators can manage roles, permissions, and recovery actions. E-tickets are generated at reservation confirmation and can be displayed as a QR code plus a human-readable ticket number; a check-in screen supports fast scanning or manual entry, shows validity windows (for example, valid 03/27/2026 9:00 AM–12:00 PM), and blocks re-entry after one successful redemption while still allowing a supervised “undo” within 2 minutes for operator mistakes with a mandatory reason entry.

The backend uses Axum to expose local REST-style APIs consumed by the Leptos frontend, with SQLite as the on-device system of record for vehicles, reservations, tickets, users, roles, and audit trails. Conflict detection must be strongly consistent under concurrency: reservation creation executes in a transaction with optimistic concurrency checks on the targeted vehicle/bay and time window, retrying up to 3 times before returning a deterministic conflict reason and alternatives computed from the same committed snapshot. Vehicle master data includes VIN, license plate, make/model/trim, photos uploaded from local files, assigned store, mileage (miles), fuel or battery percentage, upcoming maintenance/inspection/insurance expirations, and controlled status transitions (available, reserved, on-rent, in-repair, decommissioned) that require appropriate permission and are fully logged. Authentication is strictly offline: username plus password only, with passwords hashed using Argon2id and local password reset via admin-issued one-time recovery codes that expire in 30 minutes; sessions use signed tokens with a 12-hour idle timeout and CSRF protection for state-changing requests, plus standard XSS defenses through output encoding and a restrictive content policy. Security and compliance auditing is end-to-end: every create/update/delete, approval decision, permission change, export action, and ticket redemption writes a tamper-evident append-only log chain stored in SQLite with periodic hash anchors; sensitive fields (VIN, license plate, user identifiers) are masked by default in the UI and encrypted at rest using a locally managed key. File uploads are restricted to JPEG/PNG under 10 MB, validated by MIME sniffing and magic bytes, stripped of executable content, and fingerprinted to prevent duplicate storage. Backup and recovery are offline-first, producing an encrypted SQLite backup file to a user-selected local path, with restore limited to Administrators and recorded in the audit trail.

Frontend stack

modern_frontend_framework

Backend stack

not_specified

Database

SQLite"

You must choose the implementation stack strictly from the prompt itself:
1. frontend: Leptos
2. backend: Axum
3. database: SQLite
4. language: Rust for both frontend and backend

Your job is to implement a complete, structured, reviewable full-stack project that survives a static-only audit. The reviewer will not trust documentation alone. Therefore code structure, docs, security boundaries, tests, and evidence must all be statically visible and consistent.

Non-negotiable rules:
1. Use the prompt as the delivery contract.
2. Do not silently weaken, replace, or ignore any core requirement.
3. Do not implement a demo or teaching sample. Build a real product structure.
4. Do not put everything in one file.
5. Do not use mocked business behavior for core flows.
6. Do not hardcode successful auth, reservation creation, or ticket redemption.
7. Do not rely on undocumented manual setup.
8. The repo must be designed to run through Docker.
9. Keep frontend and backend clearly separated under repo/frontend and repo/backend.
10. All visible product content, docs, comments intended for reviewers, and packaged artifacts must be English only.
11. Do not include caches, editor folders, virtual environments, build junk, node_modules, helper conversion scripts, backup blobs, temp upload files, or runtime garbage in the final package.
12. Do not make unnecessary assumptions. If a business rule is unclear, record it in questions.md and choose the narrowest reasonable implementation that preserves the prompt rather than weakening it.

Important static-review expectations you must satisfy:
1. Clear startup, run, test, and configuration instructions.
2. Strong alignment between the prompt and the codebase.
3. Full end-to-end project structure rather than fragments.
4. Professional engineering structure with maintainable module boundaries.
5. Good validation, logging, API design, and error handling.
6. Explicit and reviewable security boundaries.
7. Static evidence for authorization, object access limits, and role restrictions.
8. Static evidence for tests covering the highest-risk flows.
9. Honest separation between what is proven statically and what still needs manual runtime verification.

Create the package and implement the project in this order.

PHASE 1: PACKAGE SETUP AND SOURCE-OF-TRUTH DOCS

1. Create the exact package structure shown above.
2. Create TASK-w2t76/prompt.md containing the original prompt exactly as received.
3. Create TASK-w2t76/delivery-tracker.md with:
   - current stage
   - current focus
   - hard-fail risks
   - required deliverables
   - open business ambiguities
   - packaging exclusions
4. Create TASK-w2t76/metadata.json with:
   - project_type
   - prompt
   - frontend_language
   - backend_language
   - frontend_framework
   - backend_framework
   - database
5. Set metadata values consistent with this project:
   - project_type: fullstack
   - frontend_language: rust
   - backend_language: rust
   - frontend_framework: leptos
   - backend_framework: axum
   - database: sqlite
6. Create TASK-w2t76/docs/questions.md.
7. In questions.md, use this exact format for every ambiguity:
   Question:
   My Understanding:
   Solution:
8. Only include real business-level questions such as:
   - permission scope boundaries
   - assignment visibility rules
   - store isolation rules
   - conflict suggestion tie-breaking
   - exact undo supervision model
   - backup encryption key handling assumptions
   - periodic hash anchor timing assumptions
9. Create TASK-w2t76/docs/design.md.
10. design.md must include:
   - architecture overview
   - repo/frontend and repo/backend responsibilities
   - module decomposition
   - role model
   - authentication model
   - session and CSRF model
   - authorization model
   - object-level access rules
   - reservation conflict detection model
   - optimistic concurrency and retry model
   - alternative slot suggestion model
   - alternate vehicle/bay selection model
   - ticket generation and redemption model
   - supervised undo model
   - upload validation and duplicate fingerprint model
   - vehicle lifecycle and status transition permissions
   - audit chain model
   - periodic hash anchor model
   - encryption at rest model
   - masking policy model
   - backup and restore model
   - Docker deployment model
   - logging and observability approach
11. Create TASK-w2t76/docs/api-spec.md.
12. api-spec.md must define all major endpoints with request and response shapes, auth rules, and main error cases for:
   - authentication
   - current user/session
   - password recovery code issue and reset
   - reservations
   - conflict explanations and alternatives
   - calendars and filters
   - vehicles
   - service bays
   - tickets
   - ticket redemption
   - supervised undo
   - uploads
   - exports
   - admin role and permission management
   - backup and restore
   - audit log views if applicable
13. Create TASK-w2t76/docs/test-coverage.md before coding.
14. test-coverage.md must define a risk-first coverage plan for:
   - auth success and failure
   - route authorization
   - object-level authorization
   - store isolation
   - photographer assignment isolation
   - reservation concurrency conflict
   - deterministic conflict reason generation
   - alternative slot generation
   - alternate vehicle/bay suggestion
   - ticket generation
   - ticket redemption
   - duplicate redemption blocking
   - supervised undo within 2 minutes
   - supervised undo reason required
   - vehicle status transition permissions
   - upload MIME and magic-byte validation
   - duplicate upload fingerprint rejection
   - masking of VIN/license plate/user identifiers
   - recovery code expiry
   - backup authorization
   - restore authorization
   - append-only audit chain behavior
15. Create TASK-w2t76/docs/reviewer-notes.md.
16. reviewer-notes.md must help a static reviewer by pointing to:
   - repo structure
   - auth entry points
   - security middleware
   - permission checks
   - object-level access checks
   - core business modules
   - test entry points
   - known manual verification boundaries

PHASE 2: REPO SCAFFOLD AND BACKEND FOUNDATION

1. All code must live under TASK-w2t76/repo/.
2. Create:
   - TASK-w2t76/repo/frontend/
   - TASK-w2t76/repo/backend/
   - TASK-w2t76/repo/unit_tests/
   - TASK-w2t76/repo/API_tests/
   - TASK-w2t76/repo/docker-compose.yml
   - TASK-w2t76/repo/run_tests.sh
   - TASK-w2t76/repo/README.md
3. In repo/README.md include:
   - implementation summary
   - startup instructions
   - test instructions
   - architecture summary
   - security summary
   - package structure summary
   - known limitations
   - manual verification required items
4. Build backend in repo/backend using Rust + Axum + SQLite.
5. Create a maintainable backend structure, for example:
   - src/main.rs
   - src/app/
   - src/routes/
   - src/handlers/
   - src/services/
   - src/repositories/
   - src/models/
   - src/auth/
   - src/security/
   - src/audit/
   - src/backup/
   - src/uploads/
   - src/errors/
   - migrations/
6. Implement real persistence and migrations for:
   - users
   - roles
   - permissions
   - stores
   - photographer_assignments
   - vehicles
   - service_bays
   - reservations
   - reservation_locks or versioning fields as needed
   - tickets
   - ticket_redemptions
   - recovery_codes
   - uploads
   - exports
   - backups
   - audit_log_entries
   - audit_hash_anchors
7. Implement authentication:
   - username + password only
   - Argon2id hashing
   - signed tokens or signed session tokens
   - 12-hour idle timeout behavior
   - local-only offline assumptions
8. Implement password recovery:
   - admin-issued one-time recovery codes
   - 30-minute expiry
   - auditable issue and redemption
9. Implement CSRF protection for state-changing requests.
10. Implement safe output and security controls needed by the architecture.
11. Implement authorization:
   - route-level authorization
   - function-level authorization
   - object-level authorization
   - store isolation for Merchants/Store Staff
   - assignment isolation for Photographers
   - admin-only recovery and restore actions
12. Implement reservation engine core:
   - transaction for reservation creation
   - optimistic concurrency checks
   - retry up to 3 times
   - deterministic conflict reason on final failure
   - alternatives computed from the same committed snapshot
13. Implement conflict causes:
   - overlapping reservation
   - vehicle in-repair hold
   - expired insurance
   - capacity exceeded
14. Implement alternative suggestions:
   - nearest two time slots
   - alternate eligible vehicle or bay when applicable
15. Implement vehicle master data and controlled status transitions:
   - available
   - reserved
   - on-rent
   - in-repair
   - decommissioned
16. Make status transitions permission-gated and fully audited.
17. Implement tamper-evident append-only audit chain in SQLite.
18. Implement periodic hash anchors.
19. Implement encryption at rest for sensitive fields using a locally managed key.
20. Implement masked responses by default for VIN, license plate, and user identifiers in UI-facing data.
21. Implement backup metadata and restore authorization foundations.
22. Add meaningful logs and structured error handling.
23. Add backend tests and API/integration tests for:
   - login success
   - login failure
   - invalid password or bad token behavior
   - recovery code issue and expiry
   - route authorization
   - object-level authorization
   - store isolation
   - photographer isolation
   - reservation happy path
   - reservation conflict path
   - retry logic
   - deterministic conflict reason
   - vehicle status permission enforcement
   - audit chain append behavior
   - masking behavior
   - CSRF rejection
24. Update docs and reviewer-notes as you go.

PHASE 3: FRONTEND AND CORE PRODUCT FLOWS

1. Build frontend in repo/frontend using Rust + Leptos.
2. Create a maintainable frontend structure, for example:
   - src/app.rs
   - src/routes/
   - src/pages/
   - src/components/
   - src/state/
   - src/api/
   - src/security/
   - src/utils/
3. Implement the authenticated application shell.
4. Implement role-based navigation and route guards for:
   - Customer
   - Photographer
   - Merchant/Store Staff
   - Platform Operations
   - Administrator
5. Implement unified availability calendar UI:
   - day view
   - week view
   - location filter
   - asset status filter
   - 15-minute increments
   - configurable business hours default 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM
6. Implement reservation creation UI and conflict explanation UI.
7. Show conflict reasons in plain language.
8. Show the nearest two alternative time slots.
9. Show alternate eligible vehicle or bay when applicable.
10. Implement role-specific product surfaces:
   - Customer: upcoming reservations and tickets
   - Photographer: only assignments tied to their jobs
   - Merchant/Store Staff: local fleet assets and reservations for their store
   - Platform Operations: cross-store calendars and exports
   - Administrator: roles, permissions, and recovery actions
11. Implement e-ticket UI:
   - QR code display
   - human-readable ticket number
   - validity window display
12. Implement check-in screen:
   - fast scanning or manual entry
   - valid or invalid status
   - re-entry blocked after one redemption
   - supervised undo within 2 minutes only
   - mandatory reason entry
13. Implement vehicle management UI:
   - VIN
   - license plate
   - make/model/trim
   - assigned store
   - mileage
   - fuel or battery percentage
   - maintenance/inspection/insurance expirations
   - controlled status transitions
14. Implement upload flow:
   - local file selection
   - JPEG/PNG only
   - under 10 MB
   - validation feedback
   - duplicate prevention feedback
15. Backend upload validation must include:
   - MIME sniffing
   - magic bytes
   - stripping executable content if needed by chosen approach
   - fingerprinting
16. Ensure masked sensitive fields remain masked by default in UI.
17. Do not leak unrestricted data across stores, assignments, or roles.
18. Add frontend component tests for:
   - calendar switching
   - filtering
   - reservation creation form
   - conflict explanation rendering
   - alternative suggestion rendering
   - role navigation
   - ticket display
   - duplicate redemption blocked state
   - undo time-window validation
   - undo reason validation
   - upload validation errors
19. Add API/integration tests for:
   - ticket generation
   - ticket redemption
   - duplicate redemption blocked
   - supervised undo allowed only within 2 minutes
   - supervised undo requires reason
   - upload MIME validation
   - upload magic-byte validation
   - duplicate fingerprint rejection
20. Update docs/test-coverage.md to map actual tests to actual requirements.
21. Update reviewer-notes.md with precise evidence paths.

PHASE 4: FINAL HARDENING, DOCKER, TEST ENTRYPOINTS, AND PACKAGE CLEANUP

1. Make the repo runnable through Docker.
2. Ensure repo/docker-compose.yml is present and statically coherent.
3. Ensure repo/run_tests.sh is real, documented, and orchestrates the relevant test commands.
4. Populate repo/unit_tests/ and repo/API_tests/ meaningfully.
5. Ensure docs are aligned with actual code:
   - docs/design.md matches implementation
   - docs/api-spec.md matches routes and behaviors
   - docs/questions.md contains real business ambiguities and carefully chosen solutions
   - docs/test-coverage.md includes a risk-first coverage map and honest gaps
   - docs/reviewer-notes.md points reviewers to evidence locations
6. questions.md must be clear, business-focused, and avoid unnecessary assumptions.
7. Do not invent broad assumptions when the prompt is specific.
8. Where a choice is necessary, choose the least risky interpretation that preserves the prompt.
9. Ensure the following are statically visible and easy for a reviewer to inspect:
   - auth entry points
   - token/session handling
   - CSRF protection
   - route authorization
   - object-level authorization
   - store isolation
   - photographer assignment isolation
   - reservation conflict transaction path
   - retry logic
   - deterministic conflict reasons
   - alternatives computation
   - e-ticket generation
   - redemption and re-entry block
   - supervised undo window and reason requirement
   - upload validation
   - duplicate upload prevention
   - tamper-evident audit chain
   - hash anchors
   - masking and encryption-sensitive handling
   - backup and restore authorization
10. Keep security quality high:
   - no obvious bypass paths
   - no unprotected admin actions
   - no trust in client-only validation
   - no over-broad permissions
   - no sensitive field overexposure by default
11. Keep the project professionally organized and maintainable.
12. Do not package prohibited content such as:
   - target caches not needed for source delivery
   - node_modules
   - .venv
   - .pytest_cache
   - __pycache__
   - .opencode
   - .codex
   - .vscode
   - temp upload artifacts
   - local backup files
   - conversion scripts
13. Prepare sessions/ with expected session export filenames if actual exports are not yet added.
14. Finalize metadata.json.
15. Finalize repo/README.md so a human reviewer can understand the product and inspect it statically without rewriting core code.
16. In docs/test-coverage.md, create a coverage map table with:
   - Requirement / Risk Point
   - Mapped Test Case(s)
   - Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock
   - Coverage Assessment
   - Gap
   - Minimum Test Addition
17. In reviewer-notes.md, explicitly state what still requires manual runtime verification rather than overstating it.
18. Produce the final package tree.

Output requirements for your final response:
1. show the exact final package tree
2. summarize implemented frontend areas
3. summarize implemented backend areas
4. summarize security protections
5. summarize tests added
6. summarize documentation added
7. list packaging exclusions applied
8. list honest remaining manual verification items
9. list any unresolved limitations clearly

Do not skip anything above.
Do not simplify the prompt.
Do not collapse frontend and backend into one folder.
Do not produce a sample app.
Build a real, security-conscious, review-friendly full-stack deliverable.

I'll build this complete FleetReserve Operations Suite. This is a large deliverable, so I'll work through all four phases systematically. Let me start by creating the package structure and documentation.

The project is a full-stack Rust application with Leptos frontend, Axum backend, and SQLite database for offline vehicle fleet scheduling, e-ticket admissions, and auditable multi-role access.

Create these files (write each one in full):

1. /Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/prompt.md - The original business prompt (copy from below)
2. /Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/delivery-tracker.md - Delivery tracking doc

11. /Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/sessions/bugfix-1.json - Session placeholder

ORIGINAL PROMPT TEXT for prompt.md:
"Construct a FleetReserve Operations Suite supporting offline vehicle fleet scheduling, e-ticket admissions, and auditable multi-role access for a local rental yard or dealership service center. The Leptos web UI runs in a browser on the facility network and presents a unified availability calendar that operators can switch between day/week views, filter by location and asset status, and create reservations for vehicles and related service bays in 15-minute increments within configurable business hours (default 7:00 AM-7:00 PM). When a user proposes a booking, the interface immediately explains conflicts in plain language (overlapping reservation, vehicle in-repair hold, expired insurance, or capacity exceeded) and suggests the nearest two alternative time slots and, when applicable, an alternate eligible vehicle or bay. Roles are explicit and task-oriented: Customers can sign in and view their upcoming reservations and tickets, Photographers can access only assignments tied to their jobs, Merchants/Store Staff can manage local fleet assets and reservations for their store, Platform Operations can manage cross-store calendars and exports, and Administrators can manage roles, permissions, and recovery actions. E-tickets are generated at reservation confirmation and can be displayed as a QR code plus a human-readable ticket number; a check-in screen supports fast scanning or manual entry, shows validity windows (for example, valid 03/27/2026 9:00 AM-12:00 PM), and blocks re-entry after one successful redemption while still allowing a supervised 'undo' within 2 minutes for operator mistakes with a mandatory reason entry.

The backend uses Axum to expose local REST-style APIs consumed by the Leptos frontend, with SQLite as the on-device system of record for vehicles, reservations, tickets, users, roles, and audit trails. Conflict detection must be strongly consistent under concurrency: reservation creation executes in a transaction with optimistic concurrency checks on the targeted vehicle/bay and time window, retrying up to 3 times before returning a deterministic conflict reason and alternatives computed from the same committed snapshot. Vehicle master data includes VIN, license plate, make/model/trim, photos uploaded from local files, assigned store, mileage (miles), fuel or battery percentage, upcoming maintenance/inspection/insurance expirations, and controlled status transitions (available, reserved, on-rent, in-repair, decommissioned) that require appropriate permission and are fully logged. Authentication is strictly offline: username plus password only, with passwords hashed using Argon2id and local password reset via admin-issued one-time recovery codes that expire in 30 minutes; sessions use signed tokens with a 12-hour idle timeout and CSRF protection for state-changing requests, plus standard XSS defenses through output encoding and a restrictive content policy. Security and compliance auditing is end-to-end: every create/update/delete, approval decision, permission change, export action, and ticket redemption writes a tamper-evident append-only log chain stored in SQLite with periodic hash anchors; sensitive fields (VIN, license plate, user identifiers) are masked by default in the UI and encrypted at rest using a locally managed key. File uploads are restricted to JPEG/PNG under 10 MB, validated by MIME sniffing and magic bytes, stripped of executable content, and fingerprinted to prevent duplicate storage. Backup and recovery are offline-first, producing an encrypted SQLite backup file to a user-selected local path, with restore limited to Administrators and recorded in the audit trail."

KEY REQUIREMENTS FOR EACH FILE:

**metadata.json**: 
```json
{
  "project_type": "fullstack",
  "prompt": "<the full prompt text>",
  "frontend_language": "rust",
  "backend_language": "rust", 
  "frontend_framework": "leptos",
  "backend_framework": "axum",
  "database": "sqlite"
}
```

**delivery-tracker.md**: Include current stage, current focus, hard-fail risks, required deliverables, open business ambiguities, packaging exclusions.

**questions.md**: Use exact format for each:
```
Question:
My Understanding:
Solution:
```
Include real business questions about: permission scope boundaries, assignment visibility rules, store isolation rules, conflict suggestion tie-breaking, exact undo supervision model, backup encryption key handling, periodic hash anchor timing, recovery code issuance limits, photographer assignment creation flow, export format expectations, capacity definition for service bays.

**design.md**: Must include ALL of these sections with real content:
- Architecture overview (Leptos WASM frontend + Axum REST backend + SQLite)
- repo/frontend and repo/backend responsibilities
- Module decomposition
- Role model (Customer, Photographer, Merchant/Store Staff, Platform Operations, Administrator)
- Authentication model (Argon2id, offline-only)
- Session and CSRF model (signed tokens, 12hr idle, CSRF on state-changing)
- Authorization model (route-level, function-level, object-level)
- Object-level access rules (store isolation, photographer assignment isolation)
- Reservation conflict detection model (transaction + optimistic concurrency)
- Optimistic concurrency and retry model (up to 3 retries)
- Alternative slot suggestion model (nearest 2 time slots)
- Alternate vehicle/bay selection model
- Ticket generation and redemption model (QR + human-readable number)
- Supervised undo model (2 min window, mandatory reason)
- Upload validation and duplicate fingerprint model
- Vehicle lifecycle and status transition permissions
- Audit chain model (append-only, tamper-evident)
- Periodic hash anchor model
- Encryption at rest model (locally managed key)
- Masking policy model (VIN, license plate, user IDs)
- Backup and restore model
- Docker deployment model
- Logging and observability approach

**api-spec.md**: Define ALL major endpoints with request/response shapes, auth rules, error cases for: authentication, current user/session, password recovery, reservations, conflict explanations, calendars/filters, vehicles, service bays, tickets, ticket redemption, supervised undo, uploads, exports, admin role/permission management, backup/restore, audit log views.

**test-coverage.md**: Risk-first coverage plan with coverage map table including columns: Requirement/Risk Point, Mapped Test Case(s), Key Assertion/Fixture/Mock, Coverage Assessment, Gap, Minimum Test Addition. Cover all 20+ risk areas listed in the prompt.

**reviewer-notes.md**: Point to specific file paths under repo/frontend/ and repo/backend/ for: repo structure, auth entry points, security middleware, permission checks, object-level access checks, core business modules, test entry points, known manual verification boundaries.

**Session files**: Create minimal valid JSON placeholders:
```json
{"session": "develop-1", "status": "completed", "notes": "Initial scaffold and backend foundation"}
```

Write ALL files with substantive, professional content. This is not a demo. Every doc must be detailed enough to survive a static audit. All content in English only.", "run_in_background": true}

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/prompt.md", "content": "# FleetReserve Operations Suite -- Original Business Prompt

Construct a FleetReserve Operations Suite supporting offline vehicle fleet scheduling, e-ticket admissions, and auditable multi-role access for a local rental yard or dealership service center. The Leptos web UI runs in a browser on the facility network and presents a unified availability calendar that operators can switch between day/week views, filter by location and asset status, and create reservations for vehicles and related service bays in 15-minute increments within configurable business hours (default 7:00 AM-7:00 PM). When a user proposes a booking, the interface immediately explains conflicts in plain language (overlapping reservation, vehicle in-repair hold, expired insurance, or capacity exceeded) and suggests the nearest two alternative time slots and, when applicable, an alternate eligible vehicle or bay. Roles are explicit and task-oriented: Customers can sign in and view their upcoming reservations and tickets, Photographers can access only assignments tied to their jobs, Merchants/Store Staff can manage local fleet assets and reservations for their store, Platform Operations can manage cross-store calendars and exports, and Administrators can manage roles, permissions, and recovery actions. E-tickets are generated at reservation confirmation and can be displayed as a QR code plus a human-readable ticket number; a check-in screen supports fast scanning or manual entry, shows validity windows (for example, valid 03/27/2026 9:00 AM-12:00 PM), and blocks re-entry after one successful redemption while still allowing a supervised 'undo' within 2 minutes for operator mistakes with a mandatory reason entry.

The backend uses Axum to expose local REST-style APIs consumed by the Leptos frontend, with SQLite as the on-device system of record for vehicles, reservations, tickets, users, roles, and audit trails. Conflict detection must be strongly consistent under concurrency: reservation creation executes in a transaction with optimistic concurrency checks on the targeted vehicle/bay and time window, retrying up to 3 times before returning a deterministic conflict reason and alternatives computed from the same committed snapshot. Vehicle master data includes VIN, license plate, make/model/trim, photos uploaded from local files, assigned store, mileage (miles), fuel or battery percentage, upcoming maintenance/inspection/insurance expirations, and controlled status transitions (available, reserved, on-rent, in-repair, decommissioned) that require appropriate permission and are fully logged. Authentication is strictly offline: username plus password only, with passwords hashed using Argon2id and local password reset via admin-issued one-time recovery codes that expire in 30 minutes; sessions use signed tokens with a 12-hour idle timeout and CSRF protection for state-changing requests, plus standard XSS defenses through output encoding and a restrictive content policy. Security and compliance auditing is end-to-end: every create/update/delete, approval decision, permission change, export action, and ticket redemption writes a tamper-evident append-only log chain stored in SQLite with periodic hash anchors; sensitive fields (VIN, license plate, user identifiers) are masked by default in the UI and encrypted at rest using a locally managed key. File uploads are restricted to JPEG/PNG under 10 MB, validated by MIME sniffing and magic bytes, stripped of executable content, and fingerprinted to prevent duplicate storage. Backup and recovery are offline-first, producing an encrypted SQLite backup file to a user-selected local path, with restore limited to Administrators and recorded in the audit trail.
"}

## Migrations

Create complete schema for ALL tables:

- roles (id, name, description)
- permissions (id, role_id, resource, action)

- ticket_redemptions (id, ticket_id, redeemed_by, redeemed_at, undone INTEGER DEFAULT 0, undone_at nullable, undone_by nullable, undo_reason nullable)

## Source Files

### src/main.rs
Entry point: initialize tracing, run migrations, create app state, bind Axum router on 0.0.0.0:3001.

### src/app/mod.rs + src/app/state.rs
AppState struct holding: db pool (rusqlite Connection wrapped in Arc<Mutex>), encryption key, hmac signing key, upload directory path.

### src/models/mod.rs and individual model files
Define Rust structs for ALL entities: User, Role, Permission, Store, Vehicle, ServiceBay, Reservation, Ticket, TicketRedemption, RecoveryCode, Upload, AuditLogEntry, AuditHashAnchor, Backup, PhotographerAssignment.
Also define: 
- VehicleStatus enum (Available, Reserved, OnRent, InRepair, Decommissioned)
- ReservationStatus enum
- UserRole enum
- ConflictReason enum (Overlapping, InRepairHold, ExpiredInsurance, CapacityExceeded)
- AlternativeSlot struct
- ConflictResponse struct (reasons: Vec<ConflictReason>, alternatives: Vec<AlternativeSlot>, alternate_assets: Vec<AlternateAsset>)
- MaskedVehicle, MaskedUser structs with sensitive fields masked

### src/auth/mod.rs, password.rs, session.rs, csrf.rs
- password.rs: hash_password(password) -> String using argon2id, verify_password(password, hash) -> bool
- session.rs: create_token(user_id, role, store_id) -> String using HMAC-SHA256 signed JSON payload with exp (12hr), validate_token(token) -> Option<Claims>, Claims struct with user_id, role, store_id, exp, iat, idle_timeout logic
- csrf.rs: generate_csrf_token() -> String, validate_csrf_token(token, session) -> bool

### src/security/mod.rs, headers.rs, encryption.rs, masking.rs
- headers.rs: security_headers_layer() returning tower middleware that sets CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection
- encryption.rs: encrypt_field(plaintext, key) -> String, decrypt_field(ciphertext, key) -> String using AES-256-GCM
- masking.rs: mask_vin(vin) -> String (show last 4), mask_plate(plate) -> String (show last 2), mask_username(username) -> String (show first char + ***)

### src/audit/mod.rs, chain.rs, anchors.rs
- chain.rs: append_audit_log(db, actor_id, actor_username, action, resource_type, resource_id, details) that computes hash chain (SHA-256 of previous_hash + current entry data), inserts entry
- anchors.rs: create_hash_anchor(db) that reads last anchor, computes cumulative hash of all entries since, inserts new anchor. should_create_anchor(db) -> bool based on entry count threshold (every 100 entries)

### src/services/mod.rs
Expose submodules.

### src/services/reservation_engine.rs
The CORE reservation engine:
- create_reservation(db, user_id, asset_type, asset_id, store_id, start_time, end_time) -> Result<(Reservation, Ticket), ConflictResponse>
- Implements: BEGIN TRANSACTION, check asset version, check overlapping reservations, check vehicle status (not in-repair, not decommissioned), check insurance expiry, check bay capacity, if conflict: compute alternatives from SAME snapshot (find nearest 2 free slots, find alternate eligible assets), COMMIT or ROLLBACK
- Retry logic: up to 3 attempts on version conflict (optimistic concurrency), then return deterministic ConflictResponse
- find_nearest_alternative_slots(db, asset_id, asset_type, desired_start, desired_end, store_id) -> Vec<AlternativeSlot> (nearest 2)
- find_alternate_assets(db, asset_type, store_id, start_time, end_time, exclude_id) -> Vec<AlternateAsset>

### src/services/ticket_engine.rs
- generate_ticket(db, reservation_id, valid_from, valid_until) -> Ticket
- Generate ticket_number as "FR-" + 8 uppercase alphanumeric chars
- Generate qr_data as JSON with ticket_number, valid_from, valid_until
- redeem_ticket(db, ticket_id, redeemed_by) -> Result<(), TicketError> - blocks if already redeemed
- undo_redemption(db, ticket_id, undone_by, reason) -> Result<(), TicketError> - must be within 2 min of redemption, reason required, reason must not be empty

### src/services/uploads.rs
- validate_upload(bytes, filename) -> Result<ValidatedUpload, UploadError>
- Check size <= 10MB
- Check magic bytes (JPEG: FF D8 FF, PNG: 89 50 4E 47)
- Check MIME via infer crate
- Strip EXIF/executable content (for JPEG, strip non-essential segments)
- compute_fingerprint(bytes) -> String (SHA-256 hex)
- check_duplicate(db, fingerprint) -> bool
- save_upload(db, validated, vehicle_id, store_id, uploader_id) -> Upload

### src/services/crypto.rs
- Wrapper for AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt operations
- load_or_create_key(db) -> Key
- Key rotation support structure

### src/repositories/mod.rs and individual repo files
Create repository modules for: users.rs, vehicles.rs, bays.rs, reservations.rs, tickets.rs, stores.rs, assignments.rs, recovery_codes.rs, uploads.rs, backups.rs, audit.rs
Each with standard CRUD + specialized queries. Use rusqlite for all DB access.

Key repository functions:
- users: find_by_username, find_by_id, create, update_password, list_by_store
- vehicles: find_by_id, find_by_store, update_status (with version check), create, update, list_available_in_window
- bays: find_by_id, find_by_store, check_capacity_in_window
- reservations: find_overlapping, find_by_asset_in_window, create (with version bump), find_by_user, find_by_store
- tickets: find_by_id, find_by_number, create, mark_redeemed, mark_undone
- recovery_codes: create, find_valid_by_user, mark_used
- audit: append, find_last, list_by_resource, list_by_actor

### src/handlers/mod.rs and individual handler files
Create handler modules for: auth.rs, vehicles.rs, bays.rs, reservations.rs, tickets.rs, calendar.rs, uploads.rs, admin.rs, backup.rs, assignments.rs, exports.rs

Each handler:
- Extracts authenticated user from request
- Checks authorization (role + object-level)
- Calls service/repository
- Returns JSON response
- Logs audit events for state changes

Key handlers:
- POST /api/auth/login -> returns token + csrf_token
- POST /api/auth/logout
- GET /api/auth/me -> current user
- POST /api/admin/recovery-codes -> issue recovery code (admin only)
- POST /api/auth/reset-password -> use recovery code
- GET /api/vehicles?store_id= -> list vehicles (masked by default, store-filtered)
- GET /api/vehicles/:id -> vehicle detail
- POST /api/vehicles -> create vehicle (MerchantStaff+)
- PUT /api/vehicles/:id/status -> transition status (permission-gated)
- GET /api/bays?store_id= -> list bays
- POST /api/reservations -> create reservation (runs engine)
- GET /api/reservations?user_id=&store_id= -> list reservations
- GET /api/calendar?store_id=&date=&view=day|week -> calendar data
- GET /api/tickets/:id -> ticket detail with QR
- POST /api/tickets/:id/redeem -> redeem ticket
- POST /api/tickets/:id/undo -> supervised undo
- POST /api/uploads -> multipart upload
- GET /api/exports -> export data (PlatformOps+)
- POST /api/admin/roles -> manage roles
- POST /api/admin/permissions -> manage permissions
- POST /api/backup -> create backup (admin only)
- POST /api/backup/restore -> restore backup (admin only)
- GET /api/audit?resource_type=&resource_id= -> audit log view

### src/routes/mod.rs
Build the complete Axum Router with all routes, middleware layers:
- CORS layer
- Security headers layer
- CSRF validation layer (on POST/PUT/DELETE)
- Auth extraction middleware
- Route grouping by auth requirements

### src/errors/mod.rs
AppError enum with variants for: Auth, Forbidden, NotFound, Conflict, Validation, Upload, Internal
Implement IntoResponse for AppError to return proper HTTP status codes and JSON error bodies.

### src/backup/mod.rs
- create_backup(db, path, encryption_key) -> BackupResult
- restore_backup(path, encryption_key, db) -> Result<()>
- Both operations audit-logged

## IMPORTANT IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
1. ALL code must be real, compilable Rust (not pseudo-code)
2. Use rusqlite with params![] macro for SQL queries
3. Every state-changing handler must write to audit log
4. Every handler must check authorization
5. Sensitive fields (VIN, license plate) must be encrypted at rest and masked in responses
6. The reservation engine MUST implement real transaction + retry + conflict detection
7. Ticket redemption MUST block duplicates and enforce 2-minute undo window
8. Recovery codes MUST enforce 30-minute expiry
9. Session tokens MUST enforce 12-hour idle timeout
10. Upload validation MUST check magic bytes, not just content-type header

Write every file. Do not skip any. Make the code professional, well-structured, and audit-ready.", "run_in_background": true}

- session.rs: create_token(user_id, role, store_id) -> String using HMAC-SHA256 signed JSON payload with exp (12hr), validate_token(token) -> Option<Claims>, Claims struct with user_id, role, store_id, exp, iat, idle_timeout logic
- csrf.rs: generate_csrf_token() -> String, validate_csrf_token(token, session) -> bool

### src/security/mod.rs, headers.rs, encryption.rs, masking.rs
- headers.rs: security_headers_layer() returning tower middleware that sets CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection
- encryption.rs: encrypt_field(plaintext, key) -> String, decrypt_field(ciphertext, key) -> String using AES-256-GCM
- masking.rs: mask_vin(vin) -> String (show last 4), mask_plate(plate) -> String (show last 2), mask_username(username) -> String (show first char + ***)

### src/audit/mod.rs, chain.rs, anchors.rs
- chain.rs: append_audit_log(db, actor_id, actor_username, action, resource_type, resource_id, details) that computes hash chain (SHA-256 of previous_hash + current entry data), inserts entry
- anchors.rs: create_hash_anchor(db) that reads last anchor, computes cumulative hash of all entries since, inserts new anchor. should_create_anchor(db) -> bool based on entry count threshold (every 100 entries)

### src/services/mod.rs
Expose submodules.

### src/services/reservation_engine.rs
The CORE reservation engine:
- create_reservation(db, user_id, asset_type, asset_id, store_id, start_time, end_time) -> Result<(Reservation, Ticket), ConflictResponse>
- Implements: BEGIN TRANSACTION, check asset version, check overlapping reservations, check vehicle status (not in-repair, not decommissioned), check insurance expiry, check bay capacity, if conflict: compute alternatives from SAME snapshot (find nearest 2 free slots, find alternate eligible assets), COMMIT or ROLLBACK
- Retry logic: up to 3 attempts on version conflict (optimistic concurrency), then return deterministic ConflictResponse
- find_nearest_alternative_slots(db, asset_id, asset_type, desired_start, desired_end, store_id) -> Vec<AlternativeSlot> (nearest 2)
- find_alternate_assets(db, asset_type, store_id, start_time, end_time, exclude_id) -> Vec<AlternateAsset>

### src/services/ticket_engine.rs
- generate_ticket(db, reservation_id, valid_from, valid_until) -> Ticket
- Generate ticket_number as "FR-" + 8 uppercase alphanumeric chars
- Generate qr_data as JSON with ticket_number, valid_from, valid_until
- redeem_ticket(db, ticket_id, redeemed_by) -> Result<(), TicketError> - blocks if already redeemed
- undo_redemption(db, ticket_id, undone_by, reason) -> Result<(), TicketError> - must be within 2 min of redemption, reason required, reason must not be empty

### src/services/uploads.rs
- validate_upload(bytes, filename) -> Result<ValidatedUpload, UploadError>
- Check size <= 10MB
- Check magic bytes (JPEG: FF D8 FF, PNG: 89 50 4E 47)
- Check MIME via infer crate
- Strip EXIF/executable content (for JPEG, strip non-essential segments)
- compute_fingerprint(bytes) -> String (SHA-256 hex)
- check_duplicate(db, fingerprint) -> bool
- save_upload(db, validated, vehicle_id, store_id, uploader_id) -> Upload

### src/services/crypto.rs
- Wrapper for AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt operations
- load_or_create_key(db) -> Key
- Key rotation support structure

### src/repositories/mod.rs and individual repo files
Create repository modules for: users.rs, vehicles.rs, bays.rs, reservations.rs, tickets.rs, stores.rs, assignments.rs, recovery_codes.rs, uploads.rs, backups.rs, audit.rs
Each with standard CRUD + specialized queries. Use rusqlite for all DB access.

Key repository functions:
- users: find_by_username, find_by_id, create, update_password, list_by_store
- vehicles: find_by_id, find_by_store, update_status (with version check), create, update, list_available_in_window
- bays: find_by_id, find_by_store, check_capacity_in_window
- reservations: find_overlapping, find_by_asset_in_window, create (with version bump), find_by_user, find_by_store
- tickets: find_by_id, find_by_number, create, mark_redeemed, mark_undone
- recovery_codes: create, find_valid_by_user, mark_used
- audit: append, find_last, list_by_resource, list_by_actor

### src/handlers/mod.rs and individual handler files
Create handler modules for: auth.rs, vehicles.rs, bays.rs, reservations.rs, tickets.rs, calendar.rs, uploads.rs, admin.rs, backup.rs, assignments.rs, exports.rs

Each handler:
- Extracts authenticated user from request
- Checks authorization (role + object-level)
- Calls service/repository
- Returns JSON response
- Logs audit events for state changes

Key handlers:
- POST /api/auth/login -> returns token + csrf_token
- POST /api/auth/logout
- GET /api/auth/me -> current user
- POST /api/admin/recovery-codes -> issue recovery code (admin only)
- POST /api/auth/reset-password -> use recovery code
- GET /api/vehicles?store_id= -> list vehicles (masked by default, store-filtered)
- GET /api/vehicles/:id -> vehicle detail
- POST /api/vehicles -> create vehicle (MerchantStaff+)
- PUT /api/vehicles/:id/status -> transition status (permission-gated)
- GET /api/bays?store_id= -> list bays
- POST /api/reservations -> create reservation (runs engine)
- GET /api/reservations?user_id=&store_id= -> list reservations
- GET /api/calendar?store_id=&date=&view=day|week -> calendar data
- GET /api/tickets/:id -> ticket detail with QR
- POST /api/tickets/:id/redeem -> redeem ticket
- POST /api/tickets/:id/undo -> supervised undo
- POST /api/uploads -> multipart upload
- GET /api/exports -> export data (PlatformOps+)
- POST /api/admin/roles -> manage roles
- POST /api/admin/permissions -> manage permissions
- POST /api/backup -> create backup (admin only)
- POST /api/backup/restore -> restore backup (admin only)
- GET /api/audit?resource_type=&resource_id= -> audit log view

### src/routes/mod.rs
Build the complete Axum Router with all routes, middleware layers:
- CORS layer
- Security headers layer
- CSRF validation layer (on POST/PUT/DELETE)
- Auth extraction middleware
- Route grouping by auth requirements

### src/errors/mod.rs
AppError enum with variants for: Auth, Forbidden, NotFound, Conflict, Validation, Upload, Internal
Implement IntoResponse for AppError to return proper HTTP status codes and JSON error bodies.

### src/backup/mod.rs
- create_backup(db, path, encryption_key) -> BackupResult
- restore_backup(path, encryption_key, db) -> Result<()>
- Both operations audit-logged

## IMPORTANT IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
1. ALL code must be real, compilable Rust (not pseudo-code)
2. Use rusqlite with params![] macro for SQL queries
3. Every state-changing handler must write to audit log
4. Every handler must check authorization
5. Sensitive fields (VIN, license plate) must be encrypted at rest and masked in responses
6. The reservation engine MUST implement real transaction + retry + conflict detection
7. Ticket redemption MUST block duplicates and enforce 2-minute undo window
8. Recovery codes MUST enforce 30-minute expiry
9. Session tokens MUST enforce 12-hour idle timeout
10. Upload validation MUST check magic bytes, not just content-type header

The directory structure already exists:
/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/backend/src/{app,routes,handlers,services,repositories,models,auth,security,audit,backup,uploads,errors}

/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/backend/Cargo.toml
Dependencies needed: axum 0.7, tokio, serde, serde_json, rusqlite (with bundled feature), argon2, hmac, sha2, base64, uuid, chrono, rand, hex, tower, tower-http (cors, trace), tracing, tracing-subscriber, aes-gcm (for encryption at rest), qrcode, image, infer (mime sniffing), sha2 (fingerprinting).

## Migrations
/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/backend/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql

- permissions (id, role_id, resource, action)

- service_bays (id, store_id, name, bay_type, capacity, status, version INTEGER DEFAULT 1, created_at, updated_at)

{"file_path": "/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/backend/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql", "content": "-- FleetReserve Operations Suite - Initial Schema

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS roles (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    description TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT,
    role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Customer',
    store_id INTEGER,
    active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permissions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    role_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    resource TEXT NOT NULL,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stores (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    location TEXT,
    business_hours_start TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '07:00',
    business_hours_end TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '19:00',
    active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS photographer_assignments (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    photographer_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    job_description TEXT,
    vehicle_id INTEGER,
    bay_id INTEGER,
    start_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    end_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (photographer_user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehicles(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (bay_id) REFERENCES service_bays(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vehicles (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    vin_encrypted TEXT NOT NULL,
    vin_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    license_plate_encrypted TEXT NOT NULL,
    license_plate_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    make TEXT NOT NULL,
    model TEXT NOT NULL,
    trim_level TEXT,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    mileage_miles INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    fuel_or_battery_pct INTEGER DEFAULT 100,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'available' CHECK (status IN ('available', 'reserved', 'on-rent', 'in-repair', 'decommissioned')),
    maintenance_due TEXT,
    inspection_due TEXT,
    insurance_expiry TEXT,
    version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS service_bays (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    bay_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    capacity INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
    version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reservations (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    asset_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (asset_type IN ('vehicle', 'bay')),
    asset_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    start_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    end_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    ticket_id INTEGER,
    version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (ticket_id) REFERENCES tickets(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tickets (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    ticket_number TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    reservation_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    qr_data TEXT NOT NULL,
    valid_from TEXT NOT NULL,
    valid_until TEXT NOT NULL,
    redeemed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    redeemed_at TEXT,
    redeemed_by INTEGER,
    undo_eligible_until TEXT,
    undone INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    undone_at TEXT,
    undone_by INTEGER,
    undo_reason TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (reservation_id) REFERENCES reservations(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ticket_redemptions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    ticket_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    redeemed_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
    redeemed_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    undone INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    undone_at TEXT,
    undone_by INTEGER,
    undo_reason TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (ticket_id) REFERENCES tickets(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (redeemed_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_codes (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    code_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    issued_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
    issued_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    expires_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    used INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    used_at TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (issued_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS uploads (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    filename TEXT NOT NULL,
    content_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sha256_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    vehicle_id INTEGER,
    store_id INTEGER,
    uploader_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehicles(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (uploader_id) REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_log (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    actor_id INTEGER,
    actor_username TEXT,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    resource_type TEXT,
    resource_id TEXT,
    details_json TEXT,
    previous_hash TEXT,
    current_hash TEXT NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_hash_anchors (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    anchor_time TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    last_log_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    cumulative_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS encryption_keys (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    key_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    key_material_encrypted TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS backups (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    filename TEXT NOT NULL,
    path TEXT NOT NULL,
    size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);

-- Indexes for performance
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_vehicles_store ON vehicles(store_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_vehicles_status ON vehicles(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_vehicles_vin_hash ON vehicles(vin_hash);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_asset ON reservations(asset_type, asset_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_store ON reservations(store_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_user ON reservations(user_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_time ON reservations(start_time, end_time);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tickets_number ON tickets(ticket_number);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_log_resource ON audit_log(resource_type, resource_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_log_actor ON audit_log(actor_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_uploads_fingerprint ON uploads(sha256_fingerprint);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_username ON users(username);
"}

function.name: Write
function.arguments:
{"file_path": "/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/backend/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql", "content": "-- FleetReserve Operations Suite - Initial Schema

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS roles (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    description TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT,
    role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Customer',
    store_id INTEGER,
    active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permissions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    role_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    resource TEXT NOT NULL,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stores (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    location TEXT,
    business_hours_start TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '07:00',
    business_hours_end TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '19:00',
    active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS photographer_assignments (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    photographer_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    job_description TEXT,
    vehicle_id INTEGER,
    bay_id INTEGER,
    start_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    end_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (photographer_user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehicles(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (bay_id) REFERENCES service_bays(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vehicles (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    vin_encrypted TEXT NOT NULL,
    vin_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    license_plate_encrypted TEXT NOT NULL,
    license_plate_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    make TEXT NOT NULL,
    model TEXT NOT NULL,
    trim_level TEXT,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    mileage_miles INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    fuel_or_battery_pct INTEGER DEFAULT 100,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'available' CHECK (status IN ('available', 'reserved', 'on-rent', 'in-repair', 'decommissioned')),
    maintenance_due TEXT,
    inspection_due TEXT,
    insurance_expiry TEXT,
    version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS service_bays (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    bay_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    capacity INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
    version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reservations (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    asset_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (asset_type IN ('vehicle', 'bay')),
    asset_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    store_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    start_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    end_time TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    ticket_id INTEGER,
    version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (ticket_id) REFERENCES tickets(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tickets (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    ticket_number TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    reservation_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    qr_data TEXT NOT NULL,
    valid_from TEXT NOT NULL,
    valid_until TEXT NOT NULL,
    redeemed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    redeemed_at TEXT,
    redeemed_by INTEGER,
    undo_eligible_until TEXT,
    undone INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    undone_at TEXT,
    undone_by INTEGER,
    undo_reason TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (reservation_id) REFERENCES reservations(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ticket_redemptions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    ticket_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    redeemed_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
    redeemed_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    undone INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    undone_at TEXT,
    undone_by INTEGER,
    undo_reason TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (ticket_id) REFERENCES tickets(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (redeemed_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_codes (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    code_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    issued_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
    issued_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    expires_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    used INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    used_at TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (issued_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS uploads (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    filename TEXT NOT NULL,
    content_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sha256_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    vehicle_id INTEGER,
    store_id INTEGER,
    uploader_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehicles(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (uploader_id) REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_log (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    actor_id INTEGER,
    actor_username TEXT,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    resource_type TEXT,
    resource_id TEXT,
    details_json TEXT,
    previous_hash TEXT,
    current_hash TEXT NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_hash_anchors (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    anchor_time TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    last_log_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    cumulative_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS encryption_keys (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    key_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    key_material_encrypted TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS backups (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    filename TEXT NOT NULL,
    path TEXT NOT NULL,
    size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);

-- Indexes for performance
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_vehicles_store ON vehicles(store_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_vehicles_status ON vehicles(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_vehicles_vin_hash ON vehicles(vin_hash);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_asset ON reservations(asset_type, asset_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_store ON reservations(store_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_user ON reservations(user_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reservations_time ON reservations(start_time, end_time);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tickets_number ON tickets(ticket_number);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_log_resource ON audit_log(resource_type, resource_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_log_actor ON audit_log(actor_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_uploads_fingerprint ON uploads(sha256_fingerprint);

content.name: Agent
content.arguments:
{"description": "Phase 3: Frontend Leptos code", "prompt": "Build the complete Leptos frontend for the FleetReserve Operations Suite at /Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/frontend/. This is a Rust + Leptos WASM frontend for offline vehicle fleet scheduling, e-ticket admissions, and auditable multi-role access.

The directory structure already exists:
/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/frontend/src/{routes,pages,components,state,api,security,utils}

Create ALL of the following files with REAL, compilable Rust code:

## Cargo.toml
/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/frontend/Cargo.toml
Dependencies: leptos 0.6, leptos_router 0.6, serde, serde_json, chrono, wasm-bindgen, web-sys (with features for fetch, headers, request, response, window, document, html elements, url search params), js-sys, gloo-timers, gloo-storage.

Use `[lib]` with `crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]`.

## trunk config
/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/frontend/Trunk.toml
Configure trunk to build the WASM app, with dist directory and proxy to backend at localhost:3001.

## index.html
/Users/mac/TASK-w2t76/repo/frontend/index.html
Basic HTML shell with CSP meta tag, link to trunk-generated WASM, app mount point.

## Source Files

### src/lib.rs (or src/main.rs)
Main Leptos app entry point using leptos::mount_to_body or hydrate. Set up the App component with Router.

### src/app.rs
The root App component with:
- Router setup with all routes
- Route guards based on authentication state
- Navigation structure

### src/state/mod.rs, auth.rs, app_state.rs
- auth.rs: AuthState with RwSignal holding current user info (user_id, username, role, store_id, token, csrf_token). Functions: login(), logout(), is_authenticated(), current_role(), current_store_id()
- app_state.rs: Global application state provider

### src/api/mod.rs, client.rs
- client.rs: API client that:
  - Makes fetch requests to backend at /api/*
  - Attaches Authorization header with session token
  - Attaches X-CSRF-Token header on POST/PUT/DELETE
  - Handles 401 (redirect to login), 403 (show forbidden), other errors
  - Returns deserialized JSON responses
- Define typed API functions for ALL endpoints:
  - login(username, password) -> AuthResponse
  - logout()
  - get_me() -> User
  - get_vehicles(store_id) -> Vec<MaskedVehicle>
  - get_vehicle(id) -> MaskedVehicle
  - create_vehicle(data) -> Vehicle
  - update_vehicle_status(id, status) -> Result
  - get_bays(store_id) -> Vec<ServiceBay>
  - create_reservation(data) -> Result<ReservationWithTicket, ConflictResponse>
  - get_reservations(filters) -> Vec<Reservation>
  - get_calendar(store_id, date, view) -> CalendarData
  - get_ticket(id) -> Ticket
  - redeem_ticket(id) -> Result
  - undo_redemption(id, reason) -> Result
  - upload_file(file_data) -> Result<Upload, UploadError>
  - get_exports() -> ExportData
  - issue_recovery_code(user_id) -> RecoveryCode
  - reset_password(code, new_password) -> Result
  - manage_roles/permissions
  - create_backup/restore_backup
  - get_audit_log(filters) -> Vec<AuditEntry>

### src/api/types.rs
Define all API request/response types matching the backend:
- LoginRequest, AuthResponse, User, MaskedVehicle, Vehicle, ServiceBay
- ReservationRequest, ReservationWithTicket, ConflictResponse, ConflictReason, AlternativeSlot, AlternateAsset
- Ticket, CalendarData, CalendarSlot
- Upload, UploadError, RecoveryCode
- AuditEntry, BackupInfo
- Enums: VehicleStatus, UserRole, ReservationStatus

### src/security/mod.rs, csrf.rs, route_guard.rs
- csrf.rs: store and retrieve CSRF token from auth state, attach to requests
- route_guard.rs: RouteGuard component that checks if user has required role, redirects to login or shows forbidden

### src/pages/mod.rs and individual page files

#### src/pages/login.rs
Login page with username/password form. On submit: call login API, store token + csrf_token in auth state, redirect based on role.

#### src/pages/dashboard.rs
Role-based dashboard:
- Customer: shows upcoming reservations and tickets
- Photographer: shows only assigned jobs
- MerchantStaff: shows store fleet summary
- PlatformOps: shows cross-store overview
- Administrator: shows admin panel links

#### src/pages/calendar.rs
The MAIN calendar page with:
- Day/Week view toggle buttons
- Date navigation (prev/next)
- Store/location filter dropdown
- Asset status filter (checkboxes for available, reserved, on-rent, in-repair)
- 15-minute increment time grid from business hours start to end
- Slots showing reservations with color coding by status
- Click on empty slot to start reservation creation

#### src/pages/reservations.rs
Reservation creation and management:
- Reservation form: select asset type (vehicle/bay), select asset, pick start/end time (15-min increments)
- Business hours enforcement (default 7 AM - 7 PM)
- On submit: call create_reservation API
- If conflict: show ConflictExplanation component with plain-language reasons and alternatives
- If success: show generated ticket

#### src/pages/vehicles.rs
Vehicle management page (MerchantStaff+ only):
- List vehicles for current store (or all stores for PlatformOps/Admin)
- Vehicle detail view showing: make/model/trim, masked VIN, masked plate, mileage, fuel/battery %, status, maintenance/inspection/insurance dates
- Status transition buttons (permission-gated)
- Photo upload section

#### src/pages/tickets.rs
Ticket display and check-in:
- Ticket detail with QR code display, human-readable number, validity window
- Check-in screen: text input for manual entry or scan result
- Shows valid/invalid/already-redeemed status
- Redeem button
- Undo button (visible only within 2 min of redemption, requires reason textarea)

#### src/pages/admin.rs
Admin panel:
- User management (list, create, deactivate)
- Role/permission management
- Issue recovery codes
- Backup/restore controls
- Audit log viewer with filters

#### src/pages/exports.rs
Export page (PlatformOps+):
- Cross-store data export functionality

#### src/pages/assignments.rs
Photographer assignments page:
- Shows only assignments tied to current photographer's jobs
- Assignment detail with vehicle/bay info

### src/components/mod.rs and component files

#### src/components/nav.rs
Navigation bar component:
- Shows current user info
- Role-based menu items
- Logout button

#### src/components/calendar_grid.rs
Calendar grid component:
- Renders time slots in 15-min increments
- Day view: single day column
- Week view: 7 day columns
- Color-coded reservation blocks
- Click handler for slot selection

#### src/components/conflict_explanation.rs
Conflict display component:
- Shows each conflict reason in plain language:
  - "This vehicle has an overlapping reservation from X to Y"
  - "This vehicle is currently in repair and cannot be reserved"
  - "This vehicle's insurance expired on X"
  - "This service bay is at capacity during the requested time"
- Shows "Nearest available time slots:" with 2 alternatives
- Shows "Alternative vehicles/bays:" when applicable

#### src/components/ticket_display.rs
Ticket display component:
- Shows ticket number prominently
- QR code rendered as SVG or image
- Validity window: "Valid: MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM AM - HH:MM PM"
- Status badge (valid, redeemed, expired)

#### src/components/vehicle_card.rs
Vehicle card component showing masked info, status badge, action buttons.

#### src/components/upload_form.rs
File upload component:
- File input restricted to image/jpeg, image/png
- Client-side size check (< 10MB)
- Upload progress/status
- Error display for validation failures and duplicate rejections

#### src/components/status_badge.rs
Reusable status badge component for vehicle status, reservation status, ticket status.

#### src/components/role_guard.rs
Component that conditionally renders children based on user role.

### src/utils/mod.rs, format.rs, time.rs
- format.rs: format_date, format_time, format_datetime, format_currency, format_mileage
- time.rs: round_to_15_min, generate_time_slots(start, end, increment), is_within_business_hours, time slot calculations

### src/routes/mod.rs
Route definitions matching all pages, with role-based access requirements.

## IMPORTANT:
1. All code must be real, compilable Leptos/Rust (not pseudo-code)
2. Use Leptos 0.6 patterns: #[component], view!, create_signal/RwSignal, use_context/provide_context
3. All state-changing API calls must include CSRF token
4. Sensitive data (VIN, plates) displayed only in masked form
5. Role guards on every protected route
6. Calendar must support day/week switching and 15-min increments
7. Conflict explanations must be human-readable, not error codes
8. Undo UI must enforce 2-minute window and require reason text

Write every file completely. Make the code professional and audit-ready.", "run_in_background": true}

### src/components/mod.rs and component files

#### src/components/calendar_grid.rs

#### src/components/conflict_explanation.rs

  - "This vehicle's insurance expired on X"
  - "This service bay is at capacity during the requested time"
- Shows "Nearest available time slots:" with 2 alternatives
- Shows "Alternative vehicles/bays:" when applicable

#### src/components/ticket_display.rs
